measure_plain_language: How this hospital's heart attack readmission rate compares to what would be expected given its patient mix. A ratio below 1.0 means fewer readmissions than expected.
cms_measure_definition: In October 2012, CMS began reducing Medicare payments for subsection(d) hospitals with excess readmissions. Excess readmissions are measured by a ratio, calculated by dividing a hospital's predicted rate of readmission for heart attack (AMI), heart failure (HF), pneumonia, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), hip/knee replacement (THA/TKA), and coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) surgery by the expected rate of readmission, based on an average hospital with similar patients.
